analysis.device: NVIDIA RTX PRO 6000 Blackwell Server Edition

bottleneck.description: The inductor decomposition unrolls the cuDNN RNN into one separate hidden-to-hidden gate GEMM per timestep per layer (~128 steps x 2 layers = ~256 invocations). Each is an aten::mm of shape [32,512] x [512,2048] -> [32,2048]. With M=32 the GEMM has almost no parallelism in the row dimension, so cuBLAS routes it to the small-tile SIMT kernel 'Kernel2' and then needs a 'splitKreduce_kernel' to combine the split-K partials. tensor_core_active_pct is 0.0 (smsp__pipe_tensor_cycles_active = 0): the Blackwell tensor cores sit completely idle. sm_throughput is only ~14-16% and achieved_occupancy ~12% because a 32-row GEMM cannot fill the 188 SMs. Each (Kernel2 + splitKreduce) pair costs ~8.3 us and the chain is fully serial across timesteps because timestep t+1 depends on h_t. This serial chain of tiny, tensor-core-idle GEMMs is the dominant cost (~60% of attributed time including the split-K reductions).

transformation.description: Promote the recurrent gate GEMM operands to a tensor-core-eligible dtype (bf16/fp16) so cuBLAS routes the [32,512]x[512,2048] aten::mm to the Blackwell tensor-core HMMA path instead of the FP32 SIMT 'Kernel2' kernel. This is the single highest-leverage change: the GEMM is the dominant cost, it is currently 0% tensor-core utilized, and the operand shapes are tensor-core friendly in the K=512/N=2048 dimensions. Promoting to bf16 also halves the operand memory traffic feeding the GEMM and frequently lets cuBLAS pick a tile that does not require a separate split-K reduction, eliminating the paired splitKreduce_kernel. Implement as an FX dtype-promotion pass that inserts aten._to_copy (to bf16) on the mm operands and casts the result back, keeping accumulation in fp32 inside the tensor-core MMA.
transformation.location: Aten IR graph, every torch.ops.aten.mm.default whose RHS weight is the [512,2048] recurrent gate weight (and the analogous input-projection mm); cast inserted on both mm inputs.
fx_steps: for node in gm.graph.nodes: if node.op == 'call_function' and node.target is torch.ops.aten.mm.default:
transformation.code_hint: Inserts aten._to_copy(bf16) on mm operands; cuBLAS then selects the HMMA tensor-core kernel and typically drops the splitKreduce pass. Keep the final classifier mm in fp32 if numerical accuracy of logits matters.

estimated_impact.memory_traffic_reduction: ~50% on gate GEMM operand reads (fp32->bf16); also removes ~256 splitKreduce launches

optimizations.notes: Confidence is medium not high because the win depends on cuBLAS actually choosing a tensor-core tile for M=32 (very small M sometimes still falls back to SIMT even in bf16) and on bf16 being numerically acceptable for this encoder. Duration_ns are ncu-inflated (2-5x) so treat the reduction as a relative within-profile estimate, not absolute latency. dynamic_shapes flag is set: the per-timestep cat/view kernels show high duration variance, so the 256 count is an estimate from the unroll structure (128 steps x 2 layers).

bottleneck.description: Each recurrent gate GEMM re-reads the [512,2048] weight matrix from DRAM every timestep, and cuBLAS internally transposes/repacks the weight on each call because the inductor decomposition feeds it as a plain row-major [512,2048] tensor via aten::mm. The recurrent weight is loop-invariant across all 128 timesteps, so 127 of every 128 weight loads/repacks are redundant. l2_hit_rate is only ~57% and DRAM throughput sits at ~26% while the SMs starve at 14% throughput - the GEMM is latency/launch bound on a small M, and the repeated weight handling adds avoidable traffic and a serial dependency on layout. Pre-transposing/pre-packing the recurrent weight once (hoisted out of the timestep loop) lets every timestep hit the same already-packed buffer.

transformation.description: Hoist the recurrent gate weight transpose/pack out of the per-timestep loop by replacing the per-step aten::mm(h_t, W) with a single pre-transposed constant buffer that all timesteps reuse. In Aten IR, detect the loop-invariant [512,2048] weight placeholder feeding the repeated aten::mm.default nodes, materialize W_t = aten.t / aten.permute once via register_buffer, and rewrite every recurrent mm to consume the pre-packed buffer (use aten.addmm.default folding the gate bias so the bias-add no longer needs a separate pointwise kernel). This removes redundant weight transposes and improves L2 residency since the same packed weight stays hot across timesteps.
transformation.location: Aten IR graph: the shared [512,2048] recurrent weight placeholder and all torch.ops.aten.mm.default / torch.ops.aten.addmm.default nodes that consume it across timesteps.
fx_steps: # register the transposed/packed weight as a buffer so it is computed once
for node in gm.graph.nodes: if node.op=='call_function' and node.target is torch.ops.aten.mm.default and node.args[1] is weight_node:
# fold into addmm with the gate bias and the pre-transposed weight
transformation.code_hint: Pre-transpose the recurrent weight once and fold the gate bias into aten.addmm; eliminates per-timestep weight repacking and the separate bias-add pointwise kernel.

estimated_impact.memory_traffic_reduction: Removes redundant per-timestep weight transpose traffic; improves L2 reuse of the hot [512,2048] weight

optimizations.notes: Must run AFTER OPT-1 (dtype_promotion) because register_buffer allocates the packed weight at the runtime dtype and cannot be recast after registration (general DAG rule). Confidence low: the dominant cost is the small-M GEMM math itself, so layout hoisting is a secondary win on top of OPT-1, and the exact placement of bias folding depends on how inductor laid out the gate cat. The deepest structural fix (rewriting the entire decomposed loop back into a single fused cuDNN RNN call) is out of scope for an FX Aten-IR pass.

bottleneck.description: Between every recurrent GEMM the unrolled graph emits several tiny pointwise/reshape Triton kernels: triton_poi_fused__unsafe_view (per-timestep slicing of the gate output and h/c state, ~1.6 us, grid 64-128 blocks) and triton_poi_fused_add_addmm_cat (gate bias-add + sigmoid/tanh activation + concatenation of the four gate slices, ~1.5 us). Each is launch/latency bound: grids of 64-128 blocks cannot fill 188 SMs, raw metrics are empty or near-zero for several of them, and they add ~512 extra kernel launches in the serial recurrence. These reshape/view kernels exist only because the decomposition splits the fused 4-gate tensor and re-concatenates per gate; they perform almost no real work but pay full launch latency on the critical path.

transformation.description: Fuse the per-timestep gate post-processing into a single elementwise kernel per timestep and eliminate the redundant __unsafe_view/cat reshapes. The four gate activations (sigmoid for i/f/o, tanh for g) plus the cell update c_t = f*c_{t-1} + i*g and h_t = o*tanh(c_t) should be expressed as one fused pointwise region over the [32,2048] gate tensor rather than slicing into four [32,512] views, activating each, and concatenating. In Aten IR: identify the chain aten._unsafe_view -> aten.sigmoid/aten.tanh -> aten.mul/aten.add -> aten.cat that repeats per timestep and collapse the view/cat pairs (they are layout no-ops) so inductor schedules a single fused Triton kernel over the contiguous gate buffer. This removes the ~256 __unsafe_view launches and merges the gate-activation arithmetic into one kernel.
transformation.location: Aten IR graph: per-timestep chains of torch.ops.aten._unsafe_view.default, torch.ops.aten.sigmoid.default, torch.ops.aten.tanh.default, torch.ops.aten.mul.Tensor, torch.ops.aten.add.Tensor, torch.ops.aten.cat.default.
fx_steps: for node in gm.graph.nodes: if node.op=='call_function' and node.target is torch.ops.aten._unsafe_view.default:
# _unsafe_view is a metadata-only reshape; if producer/consumer are contiguous, bypass it
# detect the sigmoid/tanh/mul/add gate chain and let inductor's pointwise scheduler fuse the contiguous region
# (no explicit re-cat: operate on slices of the single [32,2048] gate tensor in-place)
transformation.code_hint: Erase layout-noop aten._unsafe_view nodes and remove the four-gate slice/cat round-trip so inductor emits one fused pointwise kernel per timestep for the gate activations + cell update.

estimated_impact.memory_traffic_reduction: Removes redundant view/cat round-trips of the [32,2048] gate buffer; fewer global-memory passes over gate activations

optimizations.notes: Should run AFTER OPT-1 so the fused pointwise kernel operates on the bf16 gate buffer (consistent dtype) - but it does not register a buffer, so the constraint is only for dtype consistency, not a hard allocation dependency. Medium confidence: inductor already fuses some of this (kernel names show add_addmm_cat already merged), so the realizable win is mainly the ~256 __unsafe_view eliminations rather than the activation fusion which is partly done. Per-kernel raw-metric blocks are empty for many view kernels, so the per-launch duration (~1.5-1.6us) is taken from the populated siblings.

global_notes: Profile schema_version is 1.0 (validated). The profile is in raw kernel-level form: each aten op_id is a separate operator record with its own aggregated{} block. Because torch.compile UNROLLS the cuDNN RNN, the same logical op (recurrent gate GEMM) appears as ~256 distinct per-timestep operator records; the time_budget table groups these by kernel/shape family rather than by op_id, since reasoning per-op_id would produce hundreds of identical 0.3% entries.
Hundreds of sub-1% per-timestep operator records omitted from the budget table and collapsed into their kernel families (combined they ARE the budget; the grouping is the meaningful unit here).

edge_case_flag dynamic_shapes: the per-timestep triton_poi_fused cat/view kernels show >50% duration variance across same-named kernels (grid alternates 64 vs 128 blocks, duration 1376-1856 ns). The workload prompt confirms the cuDNN RNN cell emits a variable kernel count per run - kernel counts in this report are estimated from the 128-step x 2-layer unroll structure.

edge_case_flag warm_up_inflation: the warnings[] list flags ~537 leading kernels (k_00000..k_00537) as initialization kernels, and these are the EAGER cuDNN RNN path (elemWiseRNNcell, Kernel2, reduce_kernel) captured during warmup before the compiled graph runs. They are excluded from the attributed budget. Notably the eager path uses the fused cuDNN elemWiseRNNcell kernel - the compiled inductor path that replaces it with hundreds of small GEMMs is SLOWER per the decomposition, which is the core finding.

edge_case_flag high_unattributed: a large fraction of the trailing triton_poi_fused_add_addmm_cat / __unsafe_view kernels land in unattributed_kernels[] (attribution_method='unattributed', nvtx_range=null) because the prologue layer dedup range swallowed the first layer and the second layer's per-timestep ops fell outside aten:: ranges. This does not change the bottleneck conclusion since those unattributed kernels are the same per-timestep families already accounted for.

Architecture context (RTX PRO 6000 Blackwell): warp_cycles_per_instruction is null in every aggregated block (removed on Blackwell, as expected); latency-bound diagnosis therefore uses eligible_cycles_pct < ~22% plus achieved_occupancy ~12% as the indicator. tensor_core_active_pct=0.0 everywhere confirms the FP32 SIMT GEMM path - the largest structural inefficiency on a tensor-core-rich Blackwell part.

Overarching recommendation NOT expressible as a single FX pass: the fundamental problem is that torch.compile decomposes the cuDNN fused RNN into a serial chain of hundreds of tiny tensor-core-idle GEMMs. The highest-value action outside the FX-pass scope is to keep the LSTM in its cuDNN fused form (do not compile/decompose the nn.LSTM, or use a backend that preserves aten.lstm/cudnn_rnn) and only compile the mean-pool + classifier head. The FX passes above (bf16 promotion, weight pre-transpose, view/cat fusion) are the best available improvements WITHIN the already-decomposed graph.

MCP tools (context7, exa-search, memory, sequential-thinking) were unavailable in this environment, and the knowledge/ directory (hardware-limits.md, fx-patterns.md) is not present in this checkout. Per fallback rules: novel transforms capped at medium confidence, A100 limits NOT substituted since device_name was present (Blackwell). DAG ordering (OPT-1 -> OPT-2/OPT-3) constructed manually from the dtype-before-register_buffer rule.
